notes: JMS v185 shares C++ mangled symbol names with GMS v95 for all character-domain functions. The login domain differs significantly (separate opcode space, different OnCheckPasswordResult wire layout). Character domain functions are present under the same demangled names.

CLogin::OnCheckPasswordResult.notes: JMS success path (resultCode==0 or 12) differs from GMS v95. Reads: Decode4(accountId)+Decode1(nGender)+Decode1(nGradeCode)+Decode1(combined)+2×DecodeStr(nexon club IDs)+5×Decode1(flags)+DecodeBuffer(8,chatUnblockDate)+DecodeStr(extra). Fundamentally different wire layout from GMS v95. Atlas login domain tracks this separately (task-027).

calls.comment: nGradeCode (packed byte, differs from GMS v95 Decode2)
nexon club ID 1 (JMS-specific: two strings instead of one)

CLogin::OnSelectWorldResult.notes: JMS success path similar to GMS v95 but reads m_bQuerySSNOnCreateNewCharacter before m_nSlotCount and m_nBuyCharCount. Loop is fixed at 18 iterations (v95 is count-based).

calls.comment: character entry loop (18 × GW_CharacterStat::Decode + AvatarLook::Decode + onFamily + rank)

CLogin::OnWorldInformation.notes: Wire layout identical to GMS v95: Decode1(nWorldID)+DecodeStr(name)+Decode1(state)+DecodeStr(eventDesc)+Decode2+Decode2+Decode1(blockCharCreation)+Decode1(nChannelCount)+loop channels+Decode2(nBalloonCount)+loop balloons.

CLogin::OnSelectCharacterResult.notes: Success path (resultCode==0 or 12, subMode==0 or 11) reads: Decode4(ip)+Decode2(port)+Decode4(charId)+Decode1(authenCode)+Decode4(ulPremiumArgument). Same as GMS v95.

CLogin::OnCheckDuplicatedIDResult.notes: Wire layout identical to GMS v95: DecodeStr(name)+Decode1(resultCode).

CLogin::OnDeleteCharacterResult.notes: Wire layout: Decode4(dwCharacterID)+Decode1(resultCode). Identical to GMS v95.

calls.comment: result code (0=success; error codes match GMS v95)

CLogin::OnCreateNewCharacterResult.notes: Success path reads GW_CharacterStat::Decode + AvatarLook::Decode. Same structure as GMS v95 success path. Error codes differ slightly (case -24 = JMS account notice). No trailing rank/viewAll bytes.

CUser::ShowItemUpgradeEffect.notes: JMS v185 reads Decode1×5: bSuccess+bCursed+bEnchantSkill+v5+v6. NO Decode4(nEnchantCategory). enchantResultFlag (v6) IS present in JMS — same as GMS v95 position. Only enchantCategory (Decode4, GMS>87 only) is absent from JMS. The || JMS clause on the enchantCategory gate was incorrect; the || JMS clause on enchantResultFlag gate is correct.

calls.comment: characterId — consumed by CUserPool::OnUserCommonPacket dispatcher before calling this function
bEnchantSkill (JMS: no nEnchantCategory Decode4 follows this)
v6 / enchantResultFlag (display flag — present in JMS v185 even though not passed to CUIEnchantDlg)

CUser::OnEmotion.notes: JMS v185 CUser::OnEmotion@0x9f636b reads Decode4(nEmotion)+Decode4(tDuration) only — no bByItemOption. JMS v185 is older code than GMS v95; the || JMS clause on the byItemOption gate was incorrect.

calls.comment: tDuration (display duration in ms — JMS has duration but NOT byItemOption)

CWvsContext::SendEmotionChange.notes: JMS v185 SendEmotionChange@0xb0b8be encodes ONLY Encode4(charId) from the local user (ms_pInstance+252). Does NOT encode emotionId, duration, or byItemOption. Fundamentally different wire format. Atlas server opcode 0x2B reads a single int4 which JMS client sends as charId; server reinterprets as emotionId. No duration/byItemOption for JMS.

calls.comment: characterId (local user's charId from CUserLocal — NOT emotionId)

CVecCtrlUser::EndUpdateActive.notes: JMS v185 EndUpdateActive@0xaaa076 encodes Encode1(detectFlag/bDetect). If detectFlag: Encode1(fieldKey from CField+328) + Encode4(crc from CField+756) + CMovePath::Flush. NO dr0/dr1/dr2/dr3/dwKey/crc32 fields. JMS movement is GMS v83-equivalent layout. The || JMS clause on all dr-field gates was incorrect.

CWvsContext::OnCharacterInfo.notes: JMS v185 OnCharacterInfo@0xb0aa6e INCLUDES monster book block (SomethingMonsterBook call at 0xb0ac66). The gate (GMS<=87 || JMS) in info.go is CORRECT for JMS. Decode sequence differs from GMS v95: reads Decode4+Decode4 for medals (not Decode1(medalSlot)), and reads monster book before MedalAchievementInfo::Decode.

calls.comment: v32 (medal-related int32 — JMS reads Decode4 not Decode1)
monster book block (SomethingMonsterBook — present in JMS v185)

CUserLocal::OnSitResult.notes: Wire layout identical to GMS v95: Decode1(sitting)+Decode2(chairId if sitting). Confirmed by IDA.

CWvsContext::OnChangeSkillRecordResult.notes: Wire layout: Decode1(exclRequestSent)+Decode2(count)+loop(Decode4+Decode4+Decode4+DecodeBuffer(8))+Decode1(sn). Same as GMS v95.

CWvsContext::SendStatChangeRequestByItem.notes: JMS v185 appends Encode4(timeGetTime()) at end vs GMS v95. JMS sends: Encode4(updateTime)+Encode4(0x1400)+Encode2(nHP)+Encode2(nMP)+Encode1(nType)+Encode4(extraTime). The trailing int4 is ignored by atlas server. Low-severity divergence.

CWvsContext::SendCharacterInfoRequest.notes: Wire layout identical to GMS v95: Encode4(updateTime)+Encode4(dwCharacterID)+Encode1(bPetInfo).

CFuncKeyMappedMan::OnInit.notes: Wire layout same structure as GMS v95: Decode1(resetToDefault)+loop(Decode1+Decode4 per key). Loop count not determined from decompile; assumed same as v95 (90 entries). Same tool-limitation as v83/v87.

CFuncKeyMappedMan::OnPetConsumeItemInit.notes: Wire layout identical to GMS v95: Decode4(m_nPetConsumeItemID).

CFuncKeyMappedMan::OnPetConsumeMPItemIni.notes: Wire layout identical to GMS v95: Decode4(m_nPetConsumeMPItemID).

CFuncKeyMappedMan::SaveFuncKeyMap.notes: Wire layout same as GMS v95: Encode4(mode)+loop of changed key entries.

calls.comment: count (number of changed key slot indices)

CUserRemote::OnAvatarModified.notes: JMS v185 uses list format for couple/friendship: Decode1(flags)+[if bit0: AvatarLook::Decode]+[if bit1: Decode1(speed)]+[if bit2: Decode1(carryItem)]+Decode1(bCouple)+[if: Decode4(count)+loop(DecodeBuf(0x10)+Decode4(pairCharId))]+Decode1(bFriendship)+similar+Decode1(bMarriage)+[if: Decode4+Decode4+Decode4]. GMS v95 had single-entry buffers for couple/friendship. Sub-struct difference, outer flags byte identical.

calls.comment: v3 flags byte: bit0=avatarLook, bit1=speed, bit2=carryItem
couple item SN (16 bytes per entry)
friendship item SN (16 bytes per entry)

CUser::OnADBoard.notes: Wire layout identical to GMS v95: Decode1(active)+[if active: DecodeStr(message)].

calls.comment: characterId (consumed by dispatcher before calling this function)

CUserLocal::OnBalloonMsg.notes: Wire layout identical to GMS v95: DecodeStr(msg)+Decode2(width)+Decode2(duration)+Decode1(notAtPoint)+[if notAtPoint==0: Decode4(x)+Decode4(y)].

CUserLocal::OnSkillCooltimeSet.notes: Wire layout identical to GMS v95: Decode4(nSkillID)+Decode2(usRemainSec).

CWvsContext::SendAbilityUpRequest#Distri.notes: Wire layout identical to GMS v95: Encode4(updateTime)+Encode4(dwFlag).

calls.comment: nCalcDamageIndex (controlled — atlas writes for JMS too)

CMobPool::OnMobChangeController.notes: JMS v185 production shape (function size 0x47 — same as v83, no dev seed block). Matches atlas's encoder.

calls.comment: bNotChangeAction (JMS: present per atlas `|| JMS` gate)
characterId — read by CUserPool::OnUserRemotePacket before dispatch

CMob::GenerateMovePath.notes: MOB_MOVE (opcode 0xC2). Atlas MovementRequest decoder mirrors this byte-for-byte. The four `>v83 || JMS`-gated blocks in atlas (multiTargetForBall + randTimeForAreaAttack, hackedCodeCRC, bChasing/hasTarget/bChasing2/bChasingHack/tChaseDuration) all fire for JMS — verified against this decompile.

CWvsContext::OnMemoResult.notes: Wire layout identical to GMS v95: Decode1(mode)-3 dispatch (0=Display/3, 1=SendSuccess/4, 2=SendError/5, 5=Refresh/special). mode=0 (Display): Decode1(count) + loop(sub_510E70) + new-memo popup if any. Same sub-op values as GMS v95.

calls.comment: memo entry decode loop (sub_510E70 per entry)

CMemoListDlg::SetRet.notes: JMS v185 CMemoListDlg::SetRet present at 0x6c2d43. Handles memo list display from CWvsContext::OnMemoResult Display sub-op. Wire structure: reads memo list from iPacket.

calls.comment: memo list entry (called per entry from OnMemoResult Display loop)

CWvsContext::OnFriendResult#CapacityUpda.notes: JMS CWvsContext::OnFriendResult@0xb2a873. Sub-op 0x14 (CapacityUpdate): Decode1(mode) switch; case 0x14: Decode4(buddyCount). case 0x15: Decode1(capacity). cases 7/10/18: CFriend::Reset. case 8: UpdateFriend. case 9: Invite = Decode4(friendId)+DecodeStr(name)+Decode4(jobId)+Decode4(level). All sub-op values identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnFriendResult#Invite.notes: synthetic sub-mode entry: case 9 (Invite) in JMS OnFriendResult. Reads: Decode4(dwFriendID)+DecodeStr(name)+Decode4(jobId)+Decode4(level)+CFriend::Insert(). Identical wire layout to GMS v95 case 9.

CWvsContext::OnFriendResult#ListUpdate.notes: synthetic sub-mode entry: case 7/10/18 (ListUpdate/Reset) in JMS OnFriendResult. Calls CFriend::Reset(iPacket). Identical to GMS v95.

calls.comment: CFriend::Reset body — reads full buddy list from packet

CWvsContext::OnFriendResult#ChannelChang.notes: synthetic sub-mode entry: case 0x14 (20) in JMS OnFriendResult handles ChannelChange. Reads Decode4(friendId) + Decode1(channel) + Decode4(map). Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnFriendResult#Error.notes: synthetic sub-mode entry: error cases (11–15, 16, 17, 19, 22) in JMS OnFriendResult show string notices. Identical error sub-ops to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnFriendResult#Update.notes: synthetic sub-mode entry: case 8 (Update) in JMS OnFriendResult. Calls CFriend::UpdateFriend(iPacket, 1). Identical to GMS v95.

CField::SendSetFriendMsg.notes: JMS v185 CField::SendSetFriendMsg@0x56e41d. Wire layout: Encode1(mode) + EncodeStr(name) + EncodeStr(group) + Encode1(flag). Identical to GMS v95.

CField::SendAcceptFriendMsg.notes: JMS v185 CField::SendAcceptFriendMsg@0x56e66c. Wire layout: Encode1(mode=0x1) + Encode4(friendId). Identical to GMS v95.

CField::SendDeleteFriendMsg.notes: JMS v185 CField::SendDeleteFriendMsg@0x56e5bd. Wire layout: Encode1(mode=0x2) + Encode4(friendId). Identical to GMS v95.

CUIMessenger::OnPacket#Add.notes: JMS CUIMessenger::OnPacket@0x8e447e dispatches mode 0 → OnEnter. Wire: Decode1(position)+AvatarLook::Decode+DecodeStr(name)+Decode1(channelId)+Decode1(pad). Identical to GMS v95.

CUIMessenger::OnPacket#Join.notes: JMS mode 1 → OnSelfEnterResult. Wire: Decode1(position). Identical to GMS v95.

CUIMessenger::OnPacket#Remove.notes: JMS mode 2 → OnLeave. Wire: Decode1(position). Identical to GMS v95.

CUIMessenger::OnPacket#RequestInvite.notes: JMS mode 3 → OnInvite (static handler, no instance guard). Wire: DecodeStr(fromName)+Decode1(pad)+Decode4(messengerId)+Decode1(pad). Identical to GMS v95.

calls.comment: mode byte = 3 (RequestInvite) — consumed by OnPacket before calling OnInvite

CUIMessenger::OnPacket#InviteSent.notes: JMS mode 4 → OnInviteResult. Wire: DecodeStr(msg)+Decode1(success). Identical to GMS v95.

CUIMessenger::OnPacket#InviteDeclined.notes: JMS mode 5 → OnBlocked. Wire: DecodeStr(blockedUser)+Decode1(declineMode). Identical to GMS v95.

CUIMessenger::OnPacket#Chat.notes: JMS mode 6 → OnChat. Wire: DecodeStr(chatLine). Identical to GMS v95.

CUIMessenger::OnPacket#Update.notes: JMS mode 7 → OnAvatar. Wire: Decode1(position)+AvatarLook::Decode. No name/channelId/pad. Atlas Update wrongly writes name+channelId+pad — confirmed as wire bug in GMS v95 audit (GMS OnAvatar does NOT read those). JMS confirms same shape.

calls.comment: AvatarLook::Decode (no name/channelId/pad — atlas writes those incorrectly)

CUIMessenger::OnCreate.notes: JMS CUIMessenger::OnCreate (client-side handler for accepting invite). Sub-op 0 (ENTER): Encode1(0)+Encode4(messengerId). Identical to GMS v95.

CUIMessenger::OnDestroy.notes: JMS CUIMessenger::OnDestroy. Sub-op 2 (LEAVE): Encode1(2). Identical to GMS v95.

CUIMessenger::SendInviteMsg.notes: JMS CUIMessenger::SendInviteMsg. Sub-op 3 (INVITE): Encode1(3)+EncodeStr(targetCharacter). Identical to GMS v95.

CFadeWnd::SendCloseMessage.notes: JMS CFadeWnd::SendCloseMessage@0x557267. Handles decline (sub-op 5): Encode1(5)+EncodeStr(fromName)+EncodeStr(myName)+Encode1(0). Identical to GMS v95.

CUIMessenger::ProcessChat.notes: JMS CUIMessenger::ProcessChat. Sub-op 6 (CHAT): Encode1(6)+EncodeStr(chatLine). Identical to GMS v95.

CUser::OnChat.notes: JMS CUser::OnChat@0x9f5c74. Wire: Decode1(isGM)+DecodeStr(msg)+Decode1(bOnlyBalloon). Identical to GMS v95. characterId consumed by dispatcher before this function.

calls.comment: characterId — consumed by CUserPool dispatcher before OnChat

CField::SendChatMsg.notes: JMS CField::SendChatMsg@0x564a0a. Wire: Encode4(updateTime)+EncodeStr(text)+Encode1(bOnlyBalloon). Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#Created.notes: JMS CWvsContext::OnPartyResult@0xb297e7 mode=1 (self-created). Wire: Decode1(mode)+Decode4(partyId)+Decode4(memberId)+Decode4(mapId)+Decode2(jobId)+Decode2(level). Same layout as GMS v83 OnPartyResult#Created@0xa3e31c. PARTYDATA (0x12A=298 bytes small) key finding: qmemcpy(v120, &this[1].m_Cookie.szReserved[584], 0x12Au). The v95plus gate in atlas member_data.go incorrectly included JMS — bug fixed in task-066 Task 10.

calls.comment: memberId (zeros ok — atlas writes EmptyMapId as placeholder)
mapId (zeros ok — atlas writes EmptyMapId as placeholder)

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#Invite.notes: JMS case 4 (Invite): Decode4(partyId)+DecodeStr(name)+Decode4(jobId)+Decode4(level)+Decode1(autoJoin). JMS v185 DOES read jobId+level (same as GMS v87/v95). The v84plus gate in atlas invite.go (GMS > 83 || JMS) is correct.

calls.comment: jobId (present in JMS v185 — v84plus gate correct)

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#Join.notes: JMS case 7 (Join, mode=Join): Decode4(partyId)+PARTYDATA::Decode. PARTYDATA size = 0x12A=298 bytes. Case 8 (Join, mode=created): Decode4(partyId)+Decode4(charid)+Decode4(mapId)+Decode2(job)+Decode2(level)+memset(0x12A).

calls.comment: mode byte = 7 (Join with partydata) or 8 (Join newly created)
PARTYDATA::Decode (298 bytes — small format; JMS uses 0x12A not 0x17A)

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#Left.notes: JMS cases for party leave (mode ~10/11): Decode4+PARTYDATA (small, 298 bytes). JMS PARTYDATA confirmed 0x12A.

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#Update.notes: JMS case for party update (mode 33/36): Decode4(partyId)+PARTYDATA (small, 298 bytes). PARTYDATA confirmed 0x12A=298 by qmemcpy at function start.

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#Disband.notes: JMS Disband mode (mode=12, isForced=0). Same as GMS v95@0xa11085: Decode4(partyId)+Decode4(targetId)+Decode1(isForced). Atlas Disband writes mode+partyId+targetId+0x00+partyId — the trailing repeated partyId is atlas-only; pre-existing ❌ in GMS v83/v95 audits too.

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#Error.notes: JMS Error modes (mode=9/10/13/16/17/19/21/22/23/25/28/29/30/32/33/35/36). No further reads — error code only. Atlas Error writes mode+string(name), which is atlas-only extra field. Pre-existing ❌ in GMS v83/v95 audits too.

calls.comment: mode byte (error code — no further reads)

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#ChangeLeader.notes: JMS ChangeLeader: mode byte + Decode4(newLeaderId) + Decode1(hasChanged). Same as GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#MemberHP.notes: JMS CUserRemote::OnReceiveHP@0xa575be. Wire: Decode4(characterId)+Decode4(hp)+Decode4(maxHp). Same as GMS v95.

CField::SendCreateNewPartyMsg.notes: JMS CField::SendCreateNewPartyMsg@0x56ca8b. Wire: Encode1(sub-op=1). Identical to GMS v95.

CField::SendWithdrawPartyMsg.notes: JMS CField::SendWithdrawPartyMsg@0x56cba7. Wire: Encode1(sub-op=2). Identical to GMS v95.

CField::SendKickPartyMsg.notes: JMS CField::SendKickPartyMsg@0x56cf23. Wire: Encode1(sub-op=3)+EncodeStr(name). Identical to GMS v95.

CField::SendChangePartyBossMsg.notes: JMS CField::SendChangePartyBossMsg@0x56d0cc. Wire: Encode1(sub-op=5)+EncodeStr(name). Identical to GMS v95.

CField::SendJoinPartyMsg.notes: JMS CField::SendJoinPartyMsg@0x56cce9. Wire for Invite sub-op: Encode1(sub-op=4)+EncodeStr(targetName). Identical to GMS v95.

CField::SendJoinPartyMsg#OperationJoin.notes: JMS SendJoinPartyMsg OperationJoin sub-op: Encode1(sub-op=0)+Encode4(partyId)+Encode1(accepted). Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#InviteReject.notes: JMS InviteReject serverbound (same opcode as party result, client sends): Encode1(mode=5)+Encode4(partyId)+Encode1(rejected). Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult.notes: JMS CWvsContext::OnGuildResult@0xb22518. Full guild result dispatcher. Mode 5 (Invite) reads: Decode4(guildId)+DecodeStr(inviterName)+Decode4(unknown)+Decode4(skillId) — same as GMS v87/v95. The v84plus gate (GMS > 83 || JMS) in atlas operation.go is correct. Mode values and wire layouts match GMS v95 for all audited cases. BBS-related modes (above 50) absent (CUIGuildBBS not present in JMS v185).

calls.comment: mode byte — dispatches to guild sub-handler

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#RequestAgreem.notes: JMS mode 3 (RequestAgreement): Decode4(partyId)+DecodeStr(leaderName)+DecodeStr(guildName). Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#Invite.notes: JMS mode 5 (Invite): Decode4(guildId)+DecodeStr(inviterName)+Decode4(unknown)+Decode4(skillId). v84plus gate (GMS > 83 || JMS) is correct for JMS — JMS reads the full invite fields.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#ErrorMessage.notes: JMS ErrorMessage: mode byte only → string notice. Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#Disband.notes: JMS Disband: Decode4(guildId)+DecodeStr(msg). Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#MemberJoined.notes: JMS MemberJoined: Decode4(guildId) + full member entry decode. Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#MemberLeft.notes: JMS MemberLeft: Decode4(guildId)+Decode4(charId)+DecodeStr(name)+mode-specific. Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#MemberStatusU.notes: JMS MemberStatusUpdate: Decode4(guildId)+Decode4(charId)+Decode1(channel)+Decode4(job)+Decode2(level). Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#MemberTitleUp.notes: JMS MemberTitleUpdate: Decode4(guildId)+Decode4(charId)+Decode1(title). Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#EmblemChange.notes: JMS EmblemChange: Decode4(guildId)+emblem fields. Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#NoticeChange.notes: JMS NoticeChange: Decode4(guildId)+DecodeStr(notice). Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#TitleChange.notes: JMS TitleChange: Decode4(guildId)+5×DecodeStr(titles). Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#CapacityChang.notes: JMS CapacityChange (':' = 58): Decode4(guildId)+Decode1(newCapacity). If member of this guild and current member: display notice. Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#Info.notes: JMS Info ('9' = 57): guildId check + sub_510984 (guild data decode). Identical to GMS v95.

calls.comment: sub_510984 guild data decode + qmemcpy 0x24 bytes

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#AgreementResp.notes: JMS AgreementResponse (mode 3 or 28): Decode4(guildId)+DecodeStr+DecodeStr. Same as GMS v95.

CUserRemote::OnGuildNameChanged.notes: JMS CUserRemote::OnGuildNameChanged@0xa5763e. Wire: Decode4(charId, dispatcher prefix)+DecodeStr(guildName). Identical to GMS v95.

calls.comment: characterId — consumed by CUserPool dispatcher before OnGuildNameChanged

CUserRemote::OnGuildMarkChanged.notes: JMS CUserRemote::OnGuildMarkChanged@0xa57689. Wire: Decode4(charId, dispatcher prefix)+emblem fields. Identical to GMS v95.

CField::InputGuildName.notes: JMS CField::InputGuildName@0x56d98c. Wire: Encode1(sub-op=1)+EncodeStr(guildName). Identical to GMS v95.

CField::SendCreateGuildAgreeMsg.notes: JMS CField::SendCreateGuildAgreeMsg@0x56da47. Wire: Encode1(sub-op=3)+Encode1(agreed). Identical to GMS v95.

CField::SendSetGuildMarkMsg.notes: JMS CField::SendSetGuildMarkMsg@0x56e325. Wire: Encode1(sub-op=9)+Encode2(logoId)+Encode1(logoColor)+Encode2(bgId)+Encode1(bgColor). Identical to GMS v95.

CField::SendInviteGuildMsg.notes: JMS CField::SendInviteGuildMsg@0x56dab9. Wire: Encode1(sub-op=5)+EncodeStr(targetName). Identical to GMS v95.

CField::SendWithdrawGuildMsg.notes: JMS CField::SendWithdrawGuildMsg@0x56dcc7. Wire: Encode1(sub-op=7). Identical to GMS v95.

CField::SendKickGuildMsg.notes: JMS CField::SendKickGuildMsg@0x56ddf7. Wire: Encode1(sub-op=8)+EncodeStr(targetName). Identical to GMS v95.

CField::SendSetGuildNoticeMsg.notes: JMS CField::SendSetGuildNoticeMsg@0x56e3a2. Wire: Encode1(sub-op=16)+EncodeStr(notice). Identical to GMS v95.

CTabGuildAlliance::OnGradeChange.notes: JMS CTabGuildAlliance::OnGradeChange@0x9ce1c7. Present in JMS v185. Wire: Decode1(grade). Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildBBSPacket.notes: CWvsContext::OnGuildBBSPacket and all CUIGuildBBS::* functions are absent from JMS v185. BBS (Bulletin Board System) feature was not implemented in JMS v185. This is a JMS-specific omission — out of scope for atlas wire fix (no JMS client to decode BBS packets). Affected: guild/clientbound/bbs_thread_list.go, guild/clientbound/bbs_thread.go, guild/serverbound/bbs_*.go.

CUIGuildBBS::SendLoadListRequest.notes: Absent in JMS v185 — BBS not implemented. See CWvsContext::OnGuildBBSPacket note.

CUIGuildBBS::SendViewEntryRequest.notes: Absent in JMS v185 — BBS not implemented. See CWvsContext::OnGuildBBSPacket note.

CUIGuildBBS::OnGuildBBSPacket#BBSThreadL.notes: Absent in JMS v185 — BBS not implemented.

CUIGuildBBS::OnGuildBBSPacket#BBSThread.notes: Absent in JMS v185 — BBS not implemented.

CUIGuildBBS::OnCommentDelete.notes: Absent in JMS v185 — BBS not implemented.

CUIGuildBBS::OnRegister.notes: Absent in JMS v185 — BBS not implemented.

CUIGuildBBS::OnComment.notes: Absent in JMS v185 — BBS not implemented.

CUIGuildBBS::OnDelete.notes: Absent in JMS v185 — BBS not implemented.

CWvsContext::SendSetGuildTitleNames.notes: CWvsContext::SendSetGuildTitleNames absent from JMS v185. JMS uses a different title-names update mechanism. The serverbound title change uses CField::SendSetGuildMarkMsg-family rather than a dedicated WvsContext function. Out of scope — no wire bug since atlas does not send this packet to JMS clients.

CWvsContext::SendGuildJoinMsg.notes: CWvsContext::SendGuildJoinMsg absent from JMS v185. JMS uses an inline or inlined join path. Out of scope — atlas does not send guild join packets to JMS clients in the current implementation.

CFadeWnd::SendCloseMessage#DenyGuildRequ.notes: JMS CFadeWnd::SendCloseMessage@0x557267 handles DenyGuildRequest (sub-op). Wire: Encode1(sub-op)+mode-specific fields. Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#MemberExpel.notes: JMS MemberExpel ('/') — same wire as MemberLeft but with expulsion flag. Identical to GMS v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#ForeignNameCh.notes: JMS CUserRemote::OnGuildNameChanged@0xa5763e. Same as OnGuildNameChanged entry above.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#ForeignEmblem.notes: JMS CUserRemote::OnGuildMarkChanged@0xa57689. Same as OnGuildMarkChanged entry above.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#ErrorMessageW.notes: JMS ErrorMessageWithTarget: mode byte + DecodeStr(targetName). Identical to GMS v95.
